data.title: CBOT corn firms on strong demand, disease concerns
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade corn futures firmed on Friday on support from strong international demand, concerns over disease in the U.S. Midwest and technical support, analysts said.

  • Corn has been underpinned by brisk exports, concerns over Tar Spot and Southern Rust in the U.S. corn belt and technical support.

  • Ethanol producer Inpasa and Brazilian grain processor and exporter Amaggi have agreed to form a joint venture to build at least three corn-based ethanol plants in Mato Grosso, Brazil's top grain producing state, the firms said on Friday.

  • U.S. weekly corn export sales reported on Thursday topped 2 million metric tons for the second week in a row.

  • Traders were adjusting positions at the end of the month and before a long weekend in the United States, where financial markets will be closed on Monday for the Labor Day holiday.

  • CBOT December corn futures (CZ25) ended 10-1/4 cents higher at $4.20-1/4 per bushel.

data.title: Front Month ICE Brent Crude Fell 6.08% This Month to Settle at $68.12 — Data Talk
data.description:
Front Month ICE Brent Crude for Oct. delivery lost $4.41 per barrel, or 6.08% to $68.12 this month

  • Largest one month net and percentage decline since April 2025
  • Snaps a three month winning streak
  • This week it is up 39.00 cents or 0.58%
  • Up for two consecutive weeks
  • Up $2.27 or 3.45% over the last two weeks
  • Largest two week net and percentage gain since the week ending July 11, 2025
  • Today it is down 50.00 cents or 0.73%
  • Snaps a two session winning streak
  • Off 16.96% from its 52-week high of $82.03 hit Wednesday, Jan. 15, 2025
  • Up 13.10% from its 52-week low of $60.23 hit Monday, May 5, 2025
  • Down 13.55% from 52 weeks ago
  • Off 16.96% from its 2025 settlement high of $82.03 hit Wednesday, Jan. 15, 2025
  • Up 13.10% from its 2025 settlement low of $60.23 hit Monday, May 5, 2025
  • Off 53.37% from its record high of $146.08 hit Thursday, July 3, 2008
  • Year-to-date it is down $6.52 or 8.74%

All prices are calculated based on the settlement price of the current front month contract.

Source: Dow Jones Market Data

data.title: CBOT soybeans rebound as industry assesses Chinese demand
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade soybean futures chopped up and down but ended higher on technical trading on Friday, though a lack of Chinese demand for U.S. supplies hung over the oilseed market.

  • Soybean prices had climbed to a two-month peak last week, buoyed by hopes that China would revert to buying U.S. crop after months of shunning the origin in a wider trade war with Washington. But no such purchases have been confirmed.

  • Traders will be monitoring U.S.-Chinese talks in the coming days, with senior Chinese trade negotiator Li Chenggang expected in Washington.

  • Traders were adjusting positions at the end of the month and before a long weekend in the United States, where financial markets will be closed on Monday for the Labor Day holiday.

  • CBOT November soybeans (SX25) settled 6-1/2 cents higher at $10.54-1/2 per bushel.

  • CBOT December soymeal (BOZ25) ended $1.30 higher at $289 per short ton.

  • CBOT December soyoil (SMZ25) ended 0.29 cent lower at 52.14 cents per pound.

data.title: Oil Futures End Week With Modest Gains — Market Talk
data.description: Oil futures end the week slightly higher despite today's losses going into the long U.S. Labor Day weekend. Expectations of lower post-summer demand and higher OPEC+ output have offset geopolitical premium around the Russia-Ukraine war and possibility of tighter U.S. sanctions. "Refinery activity likely declines seasonally from here as crude imports are apt to increase with some of the strong OPEC production gains finding their way into the U.S.,"Ritterbusch says in a note. "The crude deficit should begin to narrow next month in reviving a price downtrend that was disrupted during the past couple of weeks by geopolitical factors." WTI settles down 0.9% at $64.01 for a 0.5% weekly gain. October Brent goes off the board at $68.12 a barrel, down 0.7% on the day and 0.6% higher than a week ago. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: PROVIDENT FINANCIAL HOLDINGS INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Provident Financial Holdings, Inc., the holding company for Provident Savings Bank, F.S.B., has released its annual Form 10-K report, providing a comprehensive overview of its financial and operational performance for the fiscal year ending June 30, 2025. Headquartered in Riverside, California, Provident Savings Bank focuses on serving consumers and small to mid-sized businesses in the Inland Empire region of Southern California. The report highlights key financial metrics, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges and risks faced by the company.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Interest Income: $35.5 million, increased by $546,000 or 2% from fiscal 2024, reflecting higher loan yields and the repricing of adjustable-rate loans.
  • Net Income: $6.3 million, down $1.1 million or 15% from fiscal 2024, primarily due to a $2.3 million increase in non-interest expense and a $410,000 decrease in non-interest income.
  • Diluted EPS: $0.93 per share, decreased from $1.06 per share in fiscal 2024.
  • Total Interest Income: $56.6 million, increased by $1.9 million or 3% from fiscal 2024, primarily due to an increase in interest income on loans receivable.
  • Total Interest Expense: $21.2 million, increased by $1.4 million or 7% from fiscal 2024, mainly due to higher interest expense on deposits.
  • Provision for (Recovery of) Credit Losses: $666,000 recovery, compared to a $63,000 recovery in fiscal 2024, due to improved qualitative factors related to single-family residential loans.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Provident Financial Holdings, Inc. operates as the holding company for Provident Savings Bank, F.S.B., a federally chartered stock savings bank headquartered in Riverside, California. The Bank focuses on serving consumers and small to mid-sized businesses in the Inland Empire region of Southern California.
  • Community Banking Operations: The Bank's primary operations include accepting deposits and originating loans, such as single-family, multi-family, and commercial real estate loans. The Bank also offers construction, commercial business, consumer, and other mortgage loans.
  • Geographical Performance: The Bank operates 12 full-service banking offices in Riverside County and one in San Bernardino County. It is the largest independent community bank headquartered in Riverside County and holds the 11th largest deposit market share in the county.
  • Market Area: The Inland Empire, with a population of approximately 4.7 million, is the Bank's primary market area. Economic growth in this region is expected to rise modestly in 2025, driven by the logistics industry and consumer credit policies.
  • Loan Portfolio Composition: As of June 30, 2025, the Bank's loan portfolio primarily consists of single-family loans (52%), multi-family loans (41%), and commercial real estate loans (7%).
  • Loan Origination and Sales: During fiscal 2025, the Bank originated $92.5 million in single-family loans and $28.9 million in multi-family and commercial real estate loans. The Bank did not purchase any loans for investment during this period.
  • Asset Quality: The Bank's non-performing assets were $1.4 million as of June 30, 2025, representing 0.11% of total assets. The Bank had no real estate owned at the end of the fiscal year.
  • Deposit Activities: The Bank's deposit portfolio includes checking, savings, money market, and time deposit accounts. Time deposits represented approximately 35% of the total deposit portfolio as of June 30, 2025.
  • Borrowing Capacity: The Bank's borrowing capacity with the FHLB – San Francisco was $504.1 million as of June 30, 2025, with $213.0 million in outstanding borrowings.
  • Future Outlook: Economic growth in the Inland Empire is expected to continue, with positive impulses from consumer credit policies and the logistics industry. The Bank anticipates maintaining its focus on community banking and loan origination in its primary market area.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: Provident aims to enhance its community banking business by moderately increasing total assets, focusing on expanding single-family, multi-family, commercial real estate, construction, and commercial business loans. The strategy includes reducing the percentage of retail time deposits while increasing lower-cost checking and savings accounts. The Corporation will consider utilizing brokered certificates of deposit and public funds to diversify its deposit funding base, subject to market conditions and funding needs.
  • Capital Management: During fiscal 2025, Provident repurchased 285,170 shares of its common stock at a weighted average cost of $15.04 per share, with 217,028 shares remaining available for purchase under the existing stock repurchase plan. The Corporation has consistently paid quarterly cash dividends on its common stock since September 30, 2002, with the current rate at $0.14 per share. The Bank maintains a strong capital position, exceeding all regulatory capital requirements, with Tier 1 Leverage Capital, CET1 Capital, Tier 1 Risk-based Capital, and Total Risk-based Capital ratios well above the minimum required to be deemed 'well capitalized.'
  • Future Outlook: Provident plans to continue its current practice of paying quarterly cash dividends, subject to the Board of Directors' discretion. The Corporation projects capital expenditures ranging from $532,000 to $1.1 million for fiscal 2026 to enhance and modernize its technology infrastructure and introduce new technology-based products. Management believes the current liquidity position is adequate to meet operational needs and regulatory requirements, supported by borrowing capacity at the FHLB – San Francisco, the FRB of San Francisco, and its correspondent bank.

Challenges and Risks

  • Geographic Concentration Risks: Provident Financial Holdings, Inc. operates primarily in Southern California, with a significant concentration of real estate loans in this region. This geographic concentration exposes the company to risks associated with local economic downturns, natural disasters, and regulatory changes specific to California. The company also faces intense competition from larger financial institutions, which may limit growth and profitability.
  • Macroeconomic Risks: The company identifies several macroeconomic risks, including potential downturns in the national and regional economies, changes in U.S. immigration policies, and global geopolitical tensions. These factors could lead to higher loan delinquencies, reduced demand for products, and increased operational risks. The company is also exposed to risks related to its lending activities, particularly in residential and commercial real estate, where changes in interest rates and property values could impact borrowers' repayment abilities.
  • Monetary Policy and Inflation Risks: Monetary policy changes, inflation, and deflation are significant risks, as they can affect the company's financial performance. The company is particularly sensitive to interest rate fluctuations, which can impact net interest income and the value of its securities portfolio. Additionally, the company faces risks related to its allowance for credit losses, which may not be sufficient to cover actual losses, especially in the event of natural disasters like wildfires.
  • Regulatory Risks: Regulatory risks are also prominent, with potential changes in accounting rules and compliance requirements impacting profitability. The company must adhere to extensive regulations, including the USA Patriot Act and Bank Secrecy Act, to prevent money laundering and terrorist financing activities.
  • Management's Discussion and Analysis: Management highlights the challenges of managing interest rate risk and liquidity, emphasizing the need for effective asset and liability management strategies. The company is also focused on maintaining a strong capital position to support operations and meet regulatory requirements. Management is aware of the potential impact of climate change on its loan portfolio and is monitoring developments in this area.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, particularly interest rate risk, which can affect the value of its loan and securities portfolios. Management employs hedging strategies to mitigate these risks, but acknowledges that these strategies may not always be effective. The company also faces risks related to cybersecurity and data breaches, which could lead to financial losses and reputational damage.

SEC Filing: PROVIDENT FINANCIAL HOLDINGS INC [ PROV ] - 10-K - Aug. 29, 2025

data.title: Greentech Innovations, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Greentech Innovations, Inc., formerly known as Startech Labs, Inc., has released its annual 10-K report, providing a comprehensive overview of its financial and operational performance for the fiscal year ended May 31, 2025. The company, which focuses on developing customized web solutions for the travel and hospitality sectors, also operates international online travel and hospitality web portals. Through its subsidiary, Analog Nest, the company is involved in the creation and development of utility and entertainment apps for mobile platforms.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 million. The company did not generate any revenues for the years ended May 31, 2025, and May 31, 2024.
  • Net Loss: $(60,650). The net loss for the year ended May 31, 2025, decreased from $(65,101) in the previous year due to a decrease in operating expenses and interest expense.
  • Net Loss Per Common Share – Basic and Diluted: $(0.10). The net loss per share improved from $(0.11) in the previous year.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Greentech Innovations, Inc. focuses on developing customized web solutions with commercial and retail applications, particularly in the travel and hospitality sectors. The company operates international online travel and hospitality web portals, allowing users to search for flights and hotels to find economical options.
  • Product Lines: Through its subsidiary Analog Nest, the company is involved in the creation and development of utility and entertainment apps for Android and iOS platforms. Analog Nest generates revenue primarily from app sales and advertising, with approximately 80% of revenue from app sales.
  • Market Opportunity: The mobile app market continues to grow, with billions of mobile devices available for app offerings. This presents significant opportunities for app developers to achieve millions of downloads, even with small app ideas.
  • Competitive Strengths: The company's apps have a strong reputation in app stores, with some ranking within the top 1,000 in their categories in the US. The apps are noted for their originality and unique source codes, distinguishing them from many copycat apps in the market.
  • Customer Base: Analog Nest has achieved approximately nine million downloads globally, with the majority of users based in the US. The company has not engaged in specific marketing efforts, relying on app store rankings and cross-promotion within its app portfolio.
  • Recent Business Development: The company has expanded its travel and metasearch offerings by launching two additional sites, www.gohotelbookings.com and www.goflightsnow.com, complementing its existing platforms. This expansion aims to enhance the "Go" branding for both leisure and business travelers.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to leverage Google's Universal app campaigns to target one million new installs in the next 12 months, requiring a marketing budget of at least $100,000 USD. This strategy aims to increase app downloads on a cost-per-install basis.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Rebranding and Restructuring: Greentech Innovations, Inc. has undergone a strategic rebranding and restructuring, including a name change from Startech Labs, Inc. to Greentech Innovations, Inc., and a reverse stock split on a 100-to-1 basis. The company is focused on developing customized web solutions, particularly in the travel and hospitality sectors, with an emphasis on fare aggregators and travel metasearch engines.
  • Capital Management: The company has not generated any revenue and has relied on director loans for operational expenses, with $29,298 advanced in 2025. It has a significant working capital deficiency of $434,363 as of May 31, 2025, primarily due to accrued interest and amounts due to related parties. The company has convertible notes totaling $129,402, which are due on demand and bear interest at 25% per annum. There were no equity repurchases or dividends declared, and the company has not adopted any equity compensation plans.
  • Future Outlook: The company faces substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern due to significant operating losses and a working capital deficiency. Management plans to rely on related party issuances, debt, and securities sales to fund operations until revenue generation begins. The company estimates total expenditures over the next 12 months to be approximately $30,000 and is exploring opportunities for additional financing to support its strategic initiatives.

Challenges and Risks

  • Competitive Environment: The company operates in the highly competitive app industry, which has low barriers to entry. This environment is expected to see more companies entering the sector, introducing a wider range of apps. The company has limited experience in developing apps for mobile and other platforms, which poses a risk to its ability to succeed against established competitors and newcomers.
  • Operational Risks: The company has not generated any revenues for the years ended May 31, 2025, and 2024, highlighting a significant operational risk related to its ability to monetize its products and services. The decrease in operating expenses and interest expenses has led to a reduced net loss, but the company still faces a working capital deficiency, primarily due to increased accrued interest and amounts due to related parties. This financial strain could impact the company's ability to sustain operations without additional financing.

SEC Filing: Greentech Innovations, Inc. [ GTIC ] - 10-K - Aug. 29, 2025

data.title: CBOT wheat ends higher on technical trading
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade wheat futures finished higher on position squaring ahead of the end of the month.

  • Traders were adjusting positions at the end of the month and before a long weekend in the United States, where financial markets will be closed on Monday for the Labor Day holiday.

  • Abundant production from Northern Hemisphere harvests and an improving outlook for crops in Southern Hemisphere exporters Argentina and Australia have weighed on wheat prices this week.

  • CBOT December soft red winter wheat (WZ25) settled 5-1/4 cents higher at $5.34-1/4 per bushel.

  • K.C. December hard red winter wheat (KWZ25) ended 4 cents higher at $5.19-3/4 per bushel.

  • Minneapolis December spring wheat (MWEZ25) ended 3 cents higher at $5.80 per bushel.

data.title: US Natgas Rises on Tight Supply, Strong Demand
data.description:
US natural gas futures rose over 2% to $3/MMBtu, marking a 7% gain for the week, supported by firming LNG feedgas flows and a below-average inventory build that reduced the storage surplus to its lowest level since the first week of June.

Analysts noted that despite this week’s rise, weather patterns over the next 15 days remain largely bearish, limiting further near-term gains.

The impact of summer heat is expected to diminish over time, which could temper future price momentum.

The tight storage levels, however, continue to underpin market sentiment, keeping prices supported.

Traders are closely monitoring LNG exports, which have provided an additional source of demand for US gas.

Overall, the combination of stronger exports and reduced inventories contributed to a bullish weekly performance.

data.title: Arabica coffee climbs back up towards recent peak
data.description:
Arabica coffee futures rose on Friday, climbing back up towards the prior session's 3-1/2-month peak, as the market remained supported by the disruption to global trade caused by the U.S. imposing a 50% tariff on imports from Brazil.

COFFEE

* Arabica coffee futures rose 2.3% at $3.861 per lb, climbing back up towards a 3-1/2-month high of $3.9130 set on Thursday.

* Arabica gained 2% this week and has gone up 34% for the last four weeks.

* Prices have been supported by speculative buying against the backdrop of a decline in exchange stocks as U.S. roasters scramble to secure supplies.

* "While the rally in robusta and arabica prices may have slowed, further price increases cannot be ruled out in the near term. Speculative activity remains influential in a low stock, low-liquidity environment," Rabobank said in a note.

* Market participants said in a Reuters poll that prices could come down at the end of the year despite the tariffs, if the outlook for better production in Brazil and Vietnam is confirmed.

* Robusta coffee futures were little changed at $4,815 a metric ton.

SUGAR

* Raw sugar ​​settled down 0.11 cent, or 0.7%, at 16.37 cents per lb. It fell 0.7% in the week.

* Sugar production in Brazil's key center-south region rose 15.96% in the first half of August compared to the year-earlier period, hitting 3.62 million metric ton, industry group UNICA said on Friday.

* Brazilian sugar maker Raizen sold two more mills as it tries to raise money to cut debt.

* White sugar was little changed at $492.70 a ton.

* The International Sugar Organization expects the 2025/26 season's supply deficit to decline sharply, driven by a rise in global production, it said on Friday.

* The intergovernmental body projected there would be a deficit of just 231,000 metric tons, down from 4.88 million in the current season.

COCOA

* London cocoa settled down 42 pounds, or 0.8%, to 5,302 pounds per ton.

* Dealers said the expiry of London September 25 options on Friday had provided a short-term focus.

* New York cocoa fell 1.3% to $7,710 a ton.

data.title: Two-year yields heading for largest monthly drop in a year
data.description:
Key points:
  • PCE data matches economists expectations for July
  • Position squaring, month end push up 10-year yields

U.S. Treasuries were mixed on Friday and interest rate-sensitive two-year yields were on track for their largest monthly drop in a year as traders squared positions ahead of Monday’s Labor Day holiday and after inflation data for July met economists’ expectations.

The Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Price Index increased 0.2% last month after an unrevised 0.3% rise in June, data on Friday showed. Excluding the volatile food and energy components, the PCE Price Index increased 0.3% last month, matching the rise in June.

The data keeps the Federal Reserve on track to cut rates at its September 16-17 meeting.

“You can check this off as one more risk to potentially derailing a cut in September. The inflation part of it, at least in this measure, is not going to do anything to reduce odds of a cut in September,” said Michael Lorizio, head of U.S. rates trading at Manulife Investment Management in Boston.

Fed funds futures traders are now pricing in 89% odds of a cut next month, up from 84% before the data.

U.S. consumer spending increased by the most in four months last month. A separate report from the University of Michigan showed consumers' one-year inflation expectations jumped to 4.8% in August from 4.5% in July.

Traders ramped up bets on more cuts after Fed Chair Jerome Powell last Friday adopted an unexpectedly dovish tone and said that risks to the job market were rising.

Efforts by President Donald Trump to fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook have raised the prospect that Trump could make more dovish appointments to the U.S. central bank that would result in easier policy.

A court hearing on Trump's attempt to fire Cook ended on Friday with no immediate ruling from the judge, meaning that Cook will remain in place for now.

Longer-dated yields edged higher on Friday as traders closed positions ahead of the long weekend and repositioned for month-end.

Some interest rate hedging was also likely influencing the market with corporate debt markets expected to pick up next week when many people return from summer vacations.

“We have a very busy week coming up ... with primary markets and all the spread product markets returning in full force, especially the corporate bond market,” said Lorizio.

Jobs data for August is also due next Friday, which may be key in determining near-term Fed policy.

The 2-year note (US2YT=RR) yield was last down 1.6 basis points on the day at 3.619%. It has fallen 33 basis points this month, the most since last August.

The yield on benchmark U.S. 10-year notes rose 1.6 basis points to 4.223%.

The yield curve between two-year and 10-year notes (US2US10=TWEB) was last at 60 basis points. It has steepened by 18 basis points this month, the most since April.

data.title: Agentic AI project Eliza Labs sues Elon Musk's xAI
data.description:
Agentic AI project Eliza Labs has filed a lawsuit against Elon Musk’s xAI, claiming that the AI company engaged in monopolistic practices meant to “deplatform” agentic AI launchpads.

The lawsuit claims that xAI attempted to “extract” valuable information, including technical documentation and usage figures related to the development of Eliza Labs’ platform, to copy its ideas before “banishing” Eliza Labs from xAI.

Eliza Labs co-founder Shaw Walters argues in the lawsuit that the relationship between the two companies started off amicably, with xAI inviting Walters to share ideas. He also said that they built on xAI’s application programming interface (API) because it was free. Walters added:

“The collaborative tone turned transactional, just as X was launching Ani and a new version of Grok. Suddenly, they were demanding we pay $50,000 a month for an enterprise license — $600,000 a year — or face legal action. 

We were already paying them over $20,000 annually through various licenses and fees,” Walters continued. Cointelegraph reached out to Walters, but he declined to provide any further comment.

The lawsuit highlights the litigious nature of the artificial intelligence sector, as legal action continues to pile up surrounding monopolistic practices, intellectual property rights, and legal liabilities of AI service providers.

Lawsuits mount in the nascent AI sector

The AI industry is still in its infancy, with key regulatory and legal questions left unanswered about the nascent technology.

These regulatory grey zones exist alongside the legal issues traditionally faced by tech companies, such as trademark and patent infringement, making the AI sector a battleground ripe for litigation.

In February 2024, Elon Musk filed a lawsuit against Sam Altman, the founder of OpenAI, and OpenAI itself, over the company’s proposal to become a for-profit enterprise.

Musk argued that the company abandoned its original mission as a non-profit, open-source project that would create tools for the public good.

The lawsuit was withdrawn several months later, in June, but was withdrawn without prejudice, meaning that Musk can continue to reintroduce the lawsuit until it is dismissed or withdrawn with prejudice.

In July 2024, The New York Times sued OpenAI over the use of copyrighted material in the company’s large-language model (LLM), ChatGPT, demanding that detailed source material be provided for AI-generated content.

Xai, an Ethereum-focused gaming company, sued Elon Musk’s xAI over trademark infringement in August 2025, alleging that the similarity of the trademarks has confused consumers and damaged its business.

data.title: Mounjaro Price Bomb Sparks UK Pharmacy Rebellion--Wegovy Cashes In
data.description:
Eli Lilly just pulled the pin on a pricing grenadeand UK pharmacies are ducking for cover. After jacking up Mounjaro's list price by as much as 170%, some pharmacies are refusing to pass the full cost onto patients. Zava, now being acquired by Hims & Hers Health, raised its highest-dose price by just 55% to 309.99, still below Lilly's new 330 benchmark. Pharmacy2U froze its prices for existing patients, even as others like Superdrug ran completely out of stock amid a nationwide shortage. Demand isn't slowing eitherone in five UK adults sought obesity meds last year, and that number's not getting smaller.

But here's the kicker: Boots is flipping Lilly's price hike into a marketing weapon. The UK giant will charge 335 for Mounjaro's top dose from September 1, but it's dangling a Switch to Wegovy offer that saves patients 129. Novo Nordisk couldn't have asked for better timing. With customer hotline wait times now stretching up to 60 minutes, the message is loud and clearLilly's move is pushing patients to reconsider their options, and Wegovy is waiting with open arms and lower prices.

Under the surface, this isn't just a price warit's a pharmacy turf battle. Asda's online doctor, which works with Zava, is slightly undercutting Zava itself. Some players are racing to lock in new customers before the September 1 reset, while others are content riding out the shortage storm. For investors, this chaos signals something deeper: pricing power in the obesity drug market may no longer sit with the drugmakers alone. Pharmacies are becoming the new gatekeepersand Wegovy just found its wedge.

data.title: Wall Street Meets the World Bank: Inside the $510M CLO Deal Shaking Up Global Finance
data.description:
The World Bank has quietly crossed a Rubicon. In a first-of-its-kind move, the development lender just packaged $510 million worth of loansmade to 57 companies across emerging marketsinto a slick Wall Street-style security known as a collateralized loan obligation (CLO). Goldman Sachs built the deal. Moody's gave its highest Aaa rating to the most senior $320 million tranche, offering a spread of 1.3 percentage points above floating rates. And the underlying borrowers? A mix of food, beverage, and telecom names spanning Eastern Europe, Latin America, and Asia. It's securitization 2.0but with a development twist.

CLOs, once radioactive after the 2008 crisis, are suddenly hot again. The global market has ballooned to over $1.3 trillion, and even retail ETFs that target US CLOs now manage north of $34 billion. The World Bank's entry into this space isn't a flukeit's strategy. By offloading loan risk to private investors, the bank frees up firepower to do more lending, faster. According to World Bank president Ajay Banga, this is just the opening act in a broader push to build an asset class around emerging-market securitizations. Expect more deals. Bigger ones. And possibly faster follow-ons tied to infrastructure and project finance.

For investors, this opens up a rare window into high-grade yield tied to growth markets. The fact that Moody's stamped the top tranche with an Aaa ratingdespite not rating the underlying loansspeaks volumes about structuring quality and portfolio diversification. The World Bank isn't reinventing the wheel. It's grafting Wall Street DNA onto development financeand doing so with partners like Goldman Sachs. In a market starved for yield and desperate for diversification, this new breed of CLO could become one of the most compelling, and quietly scalable, credit stories of the decade.

data.title: Providence Gold Mines Inc. Options La Dama de Oro Gold Property
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

    VANCOUVER – August 29, 2025 –TheNewswire - Providence Gold Mines Inc. (“Providence” or the “Company”)in addition to the news release of August 20,2025 announcement that ithas entered into an Option Agreement to acquire 100 % of the “LaDama de Oro Gold Property” from “Mohave Gold Mining”. The Optionis subject to a two percent NSR with the right for the Company to buyback one percent for $500,000 USD.

    The Option Agreement is subject to Regulatory approval.The Property is a historical gold mine 100% owned by the Optionor,(” Mohave Gold Mining”), a private Company incorporated under thelaws of the state of California.

    Providence recently commissioned Ethos Geological Inc.of Bozeman MT to complete an NI 43 101 technical report, authored byZachary Black, SME-RM acting as the Qualified Person under NI 43 101.  The NI 43 101 technical report has been submitted for Exchangereview and approval. A cautionary note: The property is at an earlyexploration stage and does not have sufficient data for a mineralresource.

    The La Dama de Oro Property is situated in the SilverMountain Mining District, within the structurally complex EasternCalifornia Shear Zone and the intersection with the San Andreas FaultZone. Bedrock geology includes Mesozoic quartz monzonite that intrudesthe Jurassic Sidewinder Volcanics. The structural history of theregion implies a sequence of compressional and extensional events thatreactivated favorably oriented zones of weakness for the circulationof hydrothermal fluids. The main zone of mineralization is hosted bythe La Dama de Oro Fault, a shallow northeast-dipping oblique-slipfault.

    The mineralization at the property is classified as astructurally controlled, low-sulfidation epithermal gold-silver veinsystem. Gold and silver mineralization is associated with multi-phasequartz veining, brecciation, and pervasive hydrothermal alterationalong the La Dama de Oro Fault. The largest known vein is 4.5 feet atits widest point and remains open to exploration, with the potentialfor additional undiscovered veins along the fault system. The propertyhas an approved exploration permit that includes a bulk sample.

    The Option entitles the Company the right to purchase100% of the La Dama de Oro Gold Property under the followingterms:

    YEAR 1

    Within 15 days of Regulatory approval the Company shallissue 2,000,000 common shares from treasury and incur $20,000 inexpenditures within 12 months of the effective date.

    YEAR 2

    The Company shall issue an additional 2,000,000 commonshares from treasury and incur $250,000 in expenditures before thesecond-year anniversary of the effective date

    YEAR 3

    The Company shall issue an additional 500,000 commonshares from treasury and incur a further $250,000 in expendituresbefore the third-year anniversary date of the effective date

    YEAR 4

    The Company shall incur an additional $250,000expenditures before the fourth-year anniversary of the effectivedate

    Mohave Gold Mines will retain a two percent NSR whichthe Company will have the right to buy back one percent for $500,000USD.

    Ronald A. Coombes, President &CEO ofProvidence commented; “The best place to explore for gold is wheregold is, with the rich historical history of past goldproductionat the La Dama de Oro mine there remains very good discoverypotential”.

    The scientific and technical information contained inthis news release has been reviewed and approved by Zachary Black,SME-RM, a Qualified Person as defined under NI 43-101. Mr. Black is aconsultant and is independent of Providence Gold Mines Inc.

     For more information, please contact Ronald Coombes,President, and CEO of the Company.

    Ronald A. Coombes, President &CE

    Phone: 604 724 2369

    roombes@providencegold.com

          CAUTIONARY STATEMENTREGARDING FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION

    Neither the OTCQB and or the TSXVenture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term isdefined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) acceptsresponsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

    All statements, trend analysis and other informationcontained in this press release relative to markets about anticipatedfuture events or results constitute forward-looking statements. Allstatements, other than statements of historical fact, included herein,including, without limitation, statements relating to the permittingprocess, future production of Providence Gold Mines, budget and timingestimates, the Company’s working capital and financing opportunitiesand statements regarding the exploration and mineralization potentialof the Company’s properties, are forward-looking statements.Forward-looking statements are subject to business and economic risksand uncertainties and other factors that could cause actual results ofoperations to differ materially from those contained in the forward-looking statements. Important factors that could cause actual resultsto differ materially from Providence Gold Mines expectations includefluctuations in commodity prices and currency exchange rates;uncertainties relating to interpretation of drill results and thegeology, continuity and grade of mineral deposits; the need forcooperation of government agencies and native groups in theexploration and development of properties and the issuance of requiredpermits; the need to obtain additional financing to develop propertiesand uncertainty as to the availability and terms of future financing;the possibility of delay in exploration or development programs anduncertainty of meeting anticipated program milestones; and uncertaintyas to timely availability of permits and other governmental approvals.Forward-looking statements are based on estimates and opinions ofmanagement at the date the statements are made. Providence Gold Minesdoes not undertake any obligation to update forward-looking statementsexcept as required by applicable securities laws. Investors should notplace undue reliance on forward-looking statement

    Copyright (c) 2025 TheNewswire - All rights reserved.

data.title: Others likely to follow Caterpillar's tariff-cost warning as prices rise
data.description:
By Steve Gelsi

Construction equipment maker lifts tariff impact estimate

Mining and construction equipment maker Caterpillar continues to face unfavorable manufacturing costs resulting from higher tariffs.

Caterpillar Inc.'s higher estimate for tariff costs is likely the first of many such warnings from companies in the manufacturing sector as increased prices of steel and aluminum impact the bottom line.

So says Raymond James analyst Tim Thein, who cut his earnings targets for Caterpillar after the bulldozer maker bumped up its cost estimates for tariffs.

"These tariffs, which apply to a number of finished goods categories with steel/aluminum content, will likely be broadly felt across the space," said Thein, who reiterated a market-perform rating on Caterpillar. "We expect these tariffs (if sustained) to put eventual downward pressure on estimates across much of our coverage."

Caterpillar's stock (CAT) fell 4.2% on Friday. The stock is one of 30 in the Dow Jones Industrial Average DJIA, which moved lower by 0.26%.

Looking ahead, Thein said he's expecting higher prices to be on the table in 2026 across the industry, but these costs will also be shaped by the pace of construction activity and dealer inventories.

Meanwhile, Oppenheimer analyst Kristen Owen reiterated an outperform rating on Caterpillar's stock, and cut its price target to $480 a share from $493 a share.

The company upped its previous tariff cost outlook it issued on Aug. 5 due to changes in steel and aluminum tariffs, as well as reciprocal rates with India, Owen said.

Caterpillar's strategy to ease the impact of tariffs remains unclear but all options are on the table, the analyst said.

All told, Caterpillar increased its third-quarter tariff cost estimate by $100 million to a range of $500 million to $600 million. Its full-year tariff cost estimate was also hiked to a range of $1.5 billion to $1.8 billion, up from an Aug. 5 projection of $1.3 billion to $1.5 billion.

As of Thursday's close, Caterpillar's stock has risen 19.9% in 2025, while the S&P 500 has risen 10.6% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average was up 7.3%.

Also read: Caterpillar expects up to $1.5 billion in tariff costs this year, and the stock is rising

-Steve Gelsi

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Tesla taps high-powered legal team to battle $243 million Autopilot verdict
data.description:
Elon Musk’s electric vehicle maker Tesla has hired a trio of prominent new lawyers as it tries to overturn a jury’s $243 million verdict in a lawsuit over the fatal crash of a Model S equipped with Tesla's Autopilot self-driving feature.

Tesla, in a filing in the federal court in Miami, Florida, on Friday asked a judge to find the jury's August 1 verdict legally unjustified and throw out the case, or to order a new trial.

Spearheading the effort are Theodore Boutrous Jr and Miguel Estrada, both top appellate lawyers at law firm Gibson Dunn, and former U.S. Solicitor General Paul Clement.

Clement is a leading advocate at the U.S. Supreme Court who has argued more than 100 cases there. Boutrous and Estrada have for years defended major companies in communications, energy and other sectors.

Tesla did not immediately respond to requests for comment. Its previous lawyers at Bowman and Brooke remain on the case as well.

In a statement, Boutrous said the Tesla "verdict is a true outlier given the facts and law and we look forward to setting things right."

Brett Schreiber, an attorney for the plaintiffs, in a statement on Friday said they were confident the court would uphold the verdict against "Tesla’s reckless and unsafe development and deployment of its Autopilot system."

The trial focused on an April 2019 crash in Key Largo that struck the victims' parked Chevrolet Tahoe as they were standing beside it. One person was killed, and another was seriously injured.

The lawsuit accused Tesla of defective design and alleged the company misled customers about Autopilot's capabilities. Tesla said the Model S driver was to blame.

Tesla told U.S. District Judge Beth Bloom that the verdict would “invite future juries to punish manufacturers who bring new safety features to market with sweeping and unpredictable tort liability.”

Jurors awarded the estate of Naibel Benavides Leon, who was killed, and her boyfriend Dillon Angulo, who was seriously injured, a combined $129 million in compensatory damages, plus $200 million in punitive damages. Tesla was held liable for 33% of the compensatory damages, or $42.6 million, and all of the punitive damages.

Tesla's new legal team argued that Florida law prohibits punitive damages in the case and challenged expert testimony presented by the plaintiffs.

“Tesla’s design and warnings, and plaintiffs’ proof, came nowhere near establishing that extraordinary level of culpability,” the filing said.

Tesla previously rejected a proposed $60 million settlement in the case.

The case is Neima Benavides v. Tesla Inc, U.S. District Court, Southern District of Florida, No. 1:21-cv-21940-BB.

Read more:

Tesla rejected $60 million settlement before losing $243 million Autopilot verdict

Autopilot verdict deals Tesla a 'black eye', threatens Musk's robotaxi ambitions

Tesla ordered by Florida jury to pay $243 million in fatal Autopilot crash

Tesla wins ruling to limit damages in US lawsuit over Autopilot crash

data.title: Brent Weaker US Demand, Ukraine Ceasefire Hopes
data.description:
Brent crude futures dropped 0.7% to settle at $68.1 per barrel on Friday, as traders weighed weaker US demand and the possibility of a ceasefire in Ukraine.

Attention is also on next week’s OPEC+ meeting, with accelerated output increases from the group raising the global supply outlook.

However, these supply gains have yet to fully reach the US market, where the summer driving season is ending, fueling concerns over demand.

Prices had risen earlier in the week following Ukrainian attacks on Russian export terminals, but reports of potential ceasefire talks eased some of that pressure.

US crude inventories showed larger-than-expected draws, indicating ongoing demand in industrial and freight sectors.

Geopolitical factors, including India’s continued purchases of Russian oil despite US tariff threats, remain under close scrutiny.

Despite Friday’s decline, Brent posted its second consecutive weekly gain, though the benchmark recorded its first monthly drop in four months.
